title: Copy VISU_MDS Machine Data Parameters From the Opposite Tipper to the Selected Axis
summary: Use the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen to copy parameters from the other tipper to the selected axis. The source states the Machine Data screen contains parameters for axes Z1, A1, Z2, and A2, and that the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button copies parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper. The same source also states these parameters generally do not require changing unless a motor is replaced.
when_to_use: Use when a source-backed need exists to copy machine data parameters from one tipper to the selected axis on the opposite tipper using the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen.
not_for: Do not use for routine operator adjustments of machine parameters.
Do not use when the reason for changing or copying parameters is unclear.
Do not use as a procedure for manual parameter editing, because the source does not provide edit, save, or confirmation details.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen, identify the selected axis, use COPY FROM OPPOSITE, and verify the selected axis shows the copied parameter set.

safety_notes: The source states these parameters do not require changing unless a motor is replaced.
Treat this as a controlled machine-parameter action and stop if the source-backed reason for copying is not established.
The source does not provide save, confirmation, or rollback details.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: VISU_MDS Machine Data screen is available for use.
A target axis is selected from Z1, A1, Z2, or A2 before using COPY FROM OPPOSITE.

external_references.quote_or_summary: The "Machine Data" screen contains all of the parameters for each axis (Z1, A1, Z2, and A2). These do not require changing unless a motor replaced. Use the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.

visual_references.description: Operator Station HMI Machine Data screen showing axis parameter settings for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2, including the VISU_MDS Machine Data layout referenced by the source.

Operator station HMI screen referenced during tipper commissioning for setting controller parameters; retrieval text notes navigation to the Visu_MDs screen using F2 and that maintenance or admin login is required to change settings.

steps.title: Open the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen
steps.purpose: Access the machine data view that contains the axis parameters used by this procedure.
steps.instruction: Open the VISU_MDS "Machine Data" screen on the operator station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The Machine Data screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The VISU_MDS Machine Data screen is visible and shows axis parameter areas for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
steps.failure_condition: The Machine Data screen cannot be opened or the expected axis parameter layout is not visible.

actions.label: Open Machine Data screen
actions.exact_action: Open the VISU_MDS "Machine Data" screen for the operator station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The VISU_MDS Machine Data view and its axis parameter layout for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
Reference for operator station HMI access to the Visu_MDs screen during controller parameter work.

stop_or_escalate_if: The VISU_MDS Machine Data screen is not accessible.
You cannot confirm you are on the Machine Data screen used for axis parameters.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Machine Data" screen contains all of the parameters for each axis (Z1, A1, Z2, and A2).
For controller parameters, the user is instructed to navigate on the operator station HMI to the "Visu_MDs" screen using F2, and must be logged in as maintenance or admin to change settings.

steps.title: Identify the selected axis
steps.purpose: Ensure the copy action is applied to the intended target axis.
steps.instruction: Identify the selected axis on the Machine Data screen from the available axes Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
steps.expected_result: A single intended target axis is identified on the screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected axis is clearly identified as one of Z1, A1, Z2, or A2 before copying.
steps.failure_condition: The target axis is unclear or cannot be confirmed on the screen.

actions.label: Confirm selected axis
actions.exact_action: Identify the selected axis on the screen from the available axes Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Axis parameter settings for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2 so the selected target axis can be identified.

stop_or_escalate_if: The intended target axis cannot be clearly identified.
There is uncertainty about which axis should receive the copied parameters.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Machine Data" screen contains all of the parameters for each axis (Z1, A1, Z2, and A2).

steps.title: Locate the COPY FROM OPPOSITE control
steps.purpose: Find the source-supported control used to transfer parameters from the other tipper.
steps.instruction: Locate the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button on the VISU_MDS Machine Data screen.
steps.expected_result: The COPY FROM OPPOSITE button is visible and ready to be used for the selected axis.
steps.healthy_condition: The COPY FROM OPPOSITE button is present on the Machine Data screen.
steps.failure_condition: The COPY FROM OPPOSITE button is not visible or cannot be identified.

actions.label: Find copy control
actions.exact_action: Locate the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button on the VISU_MDS screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The COPY FROM OPPOSITE button on the Machine Data screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The COPY FROM OPPOSITE button cannot be found on the Machine Data screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Use the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.

steps.title: Copy parameters from the opposite tipper
steps.purpose: Transfer the parameter set from the other tipper to the selected axis using the source-defined control.
steps.instruction: Use COPY FROM OPPOSITE to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.
steps.expected_result: The selected axis receives parameters from the other tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The copy action completes and the selected axis reflects the opposite tipper parameter set on the Machine Data screen.
steps.failure_condition: The copy action does not complete, the selected axis does not update, or the result cannot be confirmed on screen.

actions.label: Execute copy from opposite
actions.exact_action: Use COPY FROM OPPOSITE to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The selected axis area and the COPY FROM OPPOSITE control during the parameter copy action.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reason for changing or copying parameters is unclear.
You are being asked to manually edit parameters instead of using the documented copy function.
The source-backed copy action cannot be completed or its effect is unclear.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Use the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.

steps.title: Verify the selected axis shows the copied parameters
steps.purpose: Confirm the copy action affected the intended axis on the Machine Data screen.
steps.instruction: Verify on the Machine Data screen that the selected axis now shows the copied parameter set.
steps.expected_result: The selected axis displays the copied parameter set on the Machine Data screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected axis now shows the copied parameter values and remains identifiable as the intended target axis.
steps.failure_condition: The selected axis does not show the copied parameter set or the result cannot be verified from the screen.

actions.label: Verify copied parameter set
actions.exact_action: Verify on the Machine Data screen that the selected axis now shows the copied parameter set.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The selected axis parameter settings after using COPY FROM OPPOSITE.

stop_or_escalate_if: The selected axis does not show the expected copied parameter set.
The source does not provide enough detail to confirm save, confirmation, or persistence behavior after the copy.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The "Machine Data" screen contains all of the parameters for each axis (Z1, A1, Z2, and A2). Use the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.

success_criteria: The VISU_MDS Machine Data screen is opened.
The intended target axis is identified as one of Z1, A1, Z2, or A2.
COPY FROM OPPOSITE is used on the selected axis.
The selected axis shows the copied parameter set from the other tipper on the Machine Data screen.
healthy_conditions: The Machine Data screen displays axis parameter areas for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2.
The selected axis is clearly identifiable before the copy.
The COPY FROM OPPOSITE control is present on the screen.
After the action, the selected axis shows the copied parameter set.
failure_conditions: The VISU_MDS Machine Data screen cannot be accessed.
The intended target axis cannot be confirmed.
The COPY FROM OPPOSITE button cannot be identified or used.
The selected axis does not show the copied parameter set after the action.
The task requires manual parameter editing, save behavior, or confirmation behavior not documented in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the required source-backed reason for changing or copying parameters is unclear.
Escalate if the task appears to require manual parameter editing rather than the documented COPY FROM OPPOSITE action.
Escalate if the result cannot be verified on the Machine Data screen.
Escalate for SME review when save, confirmation, persistence, or rollback behavior is needed, because the source does not provide those details.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: VISU_MDS Machine Data screen, axis parameter layout for Z1, A1, Z2, and A2, and the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button.
Reference indicating navigation to the Visu_MDs screen on the operator station HMI during controller parameter work.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The "Machine Data" screen contains all of the parameters for each axis (Z1, A1, Z2, and A2). These do not require changing unless a motor replaced. Use the COPY FROM OPPOSITE button to copy parameters to the selected axis from the other tipper.

The source does not provide save, confirmation, or persistence details after using COPY FROM OPPOSITE.
The source does not provide rollback or recovery steps if the wrong axis is selected.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this action.
The source does not specify LOTO requirements for this action.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
